title: Methodology
subtitle: How the Brazil Elections 2026 Portal Works

disclaimer.heading: Independence Statement
disclaimer.body: This portal is an independent project with no party affiliation or electoral funding. There is no human editorial intervention: articles are collected automatically from public sources, summaries are generated by artificial intelligence, and sentiment analyses are derived algorithmically. An automated editorial feedback mechanism filters irrelevant content without manual interference. The complete source code is available for public consultation and audit at github.com/carlosduplar/eleicoes-2026-monitor.

collection.label: Collection
collection.body: RSS from 21 outlets, scraping of 8 party websites, 10 polling institutes, and YouTube monitoring, every 10 minutes via GitHub Actions. Irrelevant articles are automatically filtered by an editorial feedback mechanism that tracks blocked keywords, URLs, and sources.

summarization.label: Summarization
summarization.body: Open-source language models with hierarchical fallback: Ollama Cloud (Nemotron 3 Super, primary free) -> NVIDIA NIM (Nemotron 3 Super 120B, free) -> Ollama Cloud (MiniMax M2.5, backup) -> Vertex AI (Gemini 3 Flash Preview, paid emergency) -> MiMo V2 Flash (paid emergency). A circuit breaker and per-run limit prevent wasted calls when providers are unavailable.

sentiment.label: Sentiment
sentiment.body: Algorithmic analysis of news tone, processed in batches with per-run limits. Scores reflect the tone of headlines and summaries -- not an editorial evaluation or opinion poll.

polls.label: Election Polls
polls.body: Automatically collected from 10 institutes (Datafolha, Quaest, AtlasIntel, Parana Pesquisas, PoderData, Real Time Big Data, Futura Inteligencia, Ipsos, MDA, and Ideia). Reproduced without editorial modification.

quiz.label: Affinity Quiz
quiz.body: Positions extracted from verified statements in collected news, with confidence filtering. Sources shown in results only -- never during questions.

editorial_feedback.label: Editorial Feedback
editorial_feedback.body: Automated mechanism that tracks irrelevant articles, blocked keywords, URL patterns, and excluded sources. Articles marked as irrelevant are purged during summarization and do not appear in the public feed.

limitations.heading: Known limitations
items: AI models can make interpretation errors -- the pipeline uses multiple providers in fallback to mitigate failures.
Source coverage may have availability bias -- outlets without RSS or behind paywalls are not included.
Candidate positions evolve during the campaign; the pipeline updates daily via automated quiz.
Sentiment analysis is not equivalent to opinion polling.
Free AI providers may become temporarily unavailable -- the circuit breaker ensures continuity without waste.
YouTube monitoring uses 30-minute throttling to respect API quotas.

error_reporting.heading: Report errors
error_reporting.body: Found an error? Open an issue on the public repository.
